speaker: PANDARUS
text: Be moderate, be moderate.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Why tell you me of moderation?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: The grief is fine, full, perfect that I taste,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And violenteth in a sense as strong

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: As that which causeth it. How can I moderate it?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: If I could temporize with my affection

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Or brew it to a weak and colder palate,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: The like allayment could I give my grief.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: My love admits no qualifying dross;

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: No more my grief in such a precious loss.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Here, here, here he comes. Ah, sweet

speaker: PANDARUS
text: ducks!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: O Troilus, Troilus!

speaker: PANDARUS
text: What a pair of spectacles is here! Let me

speaker: PANDARUS
text: embrace too. “O heart,” as the goodly saying is,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: O heart, heavy heart,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Why sigh’st thou without breaking?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: where he answers again,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Because thou canst not ease thy smart

speaker: PANDARUS
text: By friendship nor by speaking.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: There was never a truer rhyme. Let us cast away

speaker: PANDARUS
text: nothing, for we may live to have need of such a

speaker: PANDARUS
text: verse. We see it, we see it. How now, lambs?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Cressid, I love thee in so strained a purity

speaker: TROILUS
text: That the blest gods, as angry with my fancy—

speaker: TROILUS
text: More bright in zeal than the devotion which

speaker: TROILUS
text: Cold lips blow to their deities—take thee from me.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Have the gods envy?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Ay, ay, ay, ay, ’tis too plain a case.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And is it true that I must go from Troy?

speaker: TROILUS
text: A hateful truth.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: What, and from Troilus too?

speaker: TROILUS
text: From Troy and Troilus.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Is ’t possible?

speaker: TROILUS
text: And suddenly, where injury of chance

speaker: TROILUS
text: Puts back leave-taking, jostles roughly by

speaker: TROILUS
text: All time of pause, rudely beguiles our lips

speaker: TROILUS
text: Of all rejoindure, forcibly prevents

speaker: TROILUS
text: Our locked embrasures, strangles our dear vows

speaker: TROILUS
text: Even in the birth of our own laboring breath.

speaker: TROILUS
text: We two, that with so many thousand sighs

speaker: TROILUS
text: Did buy each other, must poorly sell ourselves

speaker: TROILUS
text: With the rude brevity and discharge of one.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Injurious Time now with a robber’s haste

speaker: TROILUS
text: Crams his rich thiev’ry up, he knows not how.

speaker: TROILUS
text: As many farewells as be stars in heaven,

speaker: TROILUS
text: With distinct breath and consigned kisses to them,

speaker: TROILUS
text: He fumbles up into a loose adieu

speaker: TROILUS
text: And scants us with a single famished kiss,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Distasted with the salt of broken tears.

speaker: AENEAS
text: My lord, is the lady ready?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hark, you are called. Some say the genius

speaker: TROILUS
text: Cries so to him that instantly must die.—

speaker: TROILUS
text: Bid them have patience. She shall come anon.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Where are my tears? Rain, to lay this wind,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: or my heart will be blown up by the root.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I must, then, to the Grecians?

speaker: TROILUS
text: No remedy.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: A woeful Cressid ’mongst the merry Greeks.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: When shall we see again?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hear me, my love. Be thou but true of heart—

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I true? How now, what wicked deem is this?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Nay, we must use expostulation kindly,

speaker: TROILUS
text: For it is parting from us.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I speak not “Be thou true” as fearing thee,

speaker: TROILUS
text: For I will throw my glove to Death himself

speaker: TROILUS
text: That there is no maculation in thy heart;

speaker: TROILUS
text: But “Be thou true,” say I, to fashion in

speaker: TROILUS
text: My sequent protestation: “Be thou true,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And I will see thee.”

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: O, you shall be exposed, my lord, to dangers

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: As infinite as imminent! But I’ll be true.

speaker: TROILUS
text: And I’ll grow friend with danger. Wear this sleeve.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And you this glove. When shall I see you?

speaker: TROILUS
text: I will corrupt the Grecian sentinels,

speaker: TROILUS
text: To give thee nightly visitation.

speaker: TROILUS
text: But yet, be true.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: O heavens! “Be true” again?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hear why I speak it, love.

speaker: TROILUS
text: The Grecian youths are full of quality,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Their loving well composed, with gift of nature flowing,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And swelling o’er with arts and exercise.

speaker: TROILUS
text: How novelty may move, and parts with person,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Alas, a kind of godly jealousy—

speaker: TROILUS
text: Which I beseech you call a virtuous sin—

speaker: TROILUS
text: Makes me afeard.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: O heavens, you love me not!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Die I a villain then!

speaker: TROILUS
text: In this I do not call your faith in question

speaker: TROILUS
text: So mainly as my merit. I cannot sing,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Nor heel the high lavolt, nor sweeten talk,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Nor play at subtle games—fair virtues all,

speaker: TROILUS
text: To which the Grecians are most prompt and pregnant.

speaker: TROILUS
text: But I can tell that in each grace of these

speaker: TROILUS
text: There lurks a still and dumb-discursive devil

speaker: TROILUS
text: That tempts most cunningly. But be not tempted.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Do you think I will?

speaker: TROILUS
text: No.

speaker: TROILUS
text: But something may be done that we will not,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And sometimes we are devils to ourselves

speaker: TROILUS
text: When we will tempt the frailty of our powers,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Presuming on their changeful potency.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Nay, good my lord—

speaker: TROILUS
text: Come, kiss, and let us part.

speaker: PARIS
text: Brother Troilus!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Good brother, come you hither,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And bring Aeneas and the Grecian with you.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: My lord, will you be true?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Who, I? Alas, it is my vice, my fault.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Whiles others fish with craft for great opinion,

speaker: TROILUS
text: I with great truth catch mere simplicity.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Whilst some with cunning gild their copper crowns,

speaker: TROILUS
text: With truth and plainness I do wear mine bare.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Fear not my truth. The moral of my wit

speaker: TROILUS
text: Is “plain and true”; there’s all the reach of it.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Welcome, Sir Diomed. Here is the lady

speaker: TROILUS
text: Which for Antenor we deliver you.

speaker: TROILUS
text: At the port, lord, I’ll give her to thy hand

speaker: TROILUS
text: And by the way possess thee what she is.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Entreat her fair and, by my soul, fair Greek,

speaker: TROILUS
text: If e’er thou stand at mercy of my sword,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Name Cressid, and thy life shall be as safe

speaker: TROILUS
text: As Priam is in Ilium.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Fair Lady Cressid,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: So please you, save the thanks this prince expects.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: The luster in your eye, heaven in your cheek,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Pleads your fair usage, and to Diomed

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: You shall be mistress and command him wholly.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Grecian, thou dost not use me courteously,

speaker: TROILUS
text: To shame the zeal of my petition to thee

speaker: TROILUS
text: In praising her. I tell thee, lord of Greece,

speaker: TROILUS
text: She is as far high-soaring o’er thy praises

speaker: TROILUS
text: As thou unworthy to be called her servant.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I charge thee use her well, even for my charge,

speaker: TROILUS
text: For, by the dreadful Pluto, if thou dost not,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Though the great bulk Achilles be thy guard,

speaker: TROILUS
text: I’ll cut thy throat.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: O, be not moved, Prince Troilus.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Let me be privileged by my place and message

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: To be a speaker free. When I am hence,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: I’ll answer to my lust, and know you, lord,

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: I’ll nothing do on charge. To her own worth

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: She shall be prized; but that you say “Be ’t so,”

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: I speak it in my spirit and honor: “no.”

speaker: TROILUS
text: Come, to the port. I’ll tell thee, Diomed,

speaker: TROILUS
text: This brave shall oft make thee to hide thy head.—

speaker: TROILUS
text: Lady, give me your hand, and, as we walk,

speaker: TROILUS
text: To our own selves bend we our needful talk.

speaker: PARIS
text: Hark, Hector’s trumpet.

speaker: AENEAS
text: How have we spent this morning!

speaker: AENEAS
text: The Prince must think me tardy and remiss

speaker: AENEAS
text: That swore to ride before him to the field.

speaker: PARIS
text: ’Tis Troilus’ fault. Come, come to field with him.

speaker: DEIPHOBUS
text: Let us make ready straight.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Yea, with a bridegroom’s fresh alacrity

speaker: AENEAS
text: Let us address to tend on Hector’s heels.

speaker: AENEAS
text: The glory of our Troy doth this day lie

speaker: AENEAS
text: On his fair worth and single chivalry.
